channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel Strikes Southern Lebanon Despite Partial Hezbollah Truce
item.description: Israel continues military operations in southern Lebanon though a partial ceasefire brokered by the US appears to be holding in Beirut. The conflict enters its third month with no clear diplomatic resolution in sight.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Seven Civilians Killed as Drone Strikes Bus in Russia-Controlled Ukraine
item.description: A bus traveling between Moscow and Simferopol in Russian-occupied Crimea was hit by a drone attack, killing seven people. The incident underscores the ongoing dangers to civilian infrastructure on both sides of the conflict.

item.title: 🇰🇪🇺🇸🏥 Kenya Court Deals New Setback to US Ebola Quarantine Unit Plan
item.description: A Kenyan court has further delayed the Trump administration's proposed Ebola quarantine facility for Americans exposed to the virus. The plan has sparked angry protests among Kenyans who fear exposure to the disease.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Strikes St. Petersburg Oil Terminal on Putin Economic Forum Opening Day
item.description: Ukrainian forces attacked a major oil terminal in St. Petersburg, coinciding with the opening of Putin's International Economic Forum, demonstrating Ukraine's ability to strike deep into Russian territory.

item.title: ☢️ US May Place Nuclear Weapons in Poland or Baltic States, Report Says
item.description: The US is considering expanding nuclear hosting arrangements in Eastern Europe as trust in American security guarantees wavers, though officials stress no agreement is imminent.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇮🇱 France Restricts Israeli Presence at Europe's Largest Defense Show
item.description: Paris has limited Israeli participation in Eurosatory amid strained relations over Israel's military operations in Lebanon, marking a significant diplomatic shift.

item.title: 🇧🇭 Bahrain Intercepts Iranian Missiles and Drones as Gulf Hostilities Flare
item.description: Bahrain's military announced it intercepted Iranian drones and missiles amid escalating regional tensions, while the US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Advances Nuclear-Powered Submarine Program
item.description: Seoul is pushing forward with plans for nuclear-powered submarines, marking a major shift in South Korean naval strategy and capabilities.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Seven Killed After Drone Hits Bus in Russia-Controlled Ukraine
item.description: A drone struck a bus traveling between Moscow and Simferopol in Russian-occupied Crimea, killing seven people. The attack highlights the ongoing dangers to civilian transport in the conflict zone.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Shell Pumped Oil Through Nigeria Pipeline Despite Pollution Evidence
item.description: Documents reveal Shell continued pumping oil through a Nigerian pipeline for years despite internal evidence of significant pollution, raising serious environmental and corporate accountability concerns.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iran-Backed Militias in Iraq to Start Handing Over Weapons
item.description: Powerful Iran-backed militias in Iraq announced they will begin handing in weapons to authorities, a significant development amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇧 British Couple Lose Iran Jail Sentence Appeal
item.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman, arrested in January 2025 during a global motorbike journey, have lost their appeal against convictions in Iran.

item.title: 🇺🇦🚢💰 World's Largest Shipping Firm MSC Invests in Ukrainian Port Near Odesa
item.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company, controlling roughly a fifth of global container traffic, acquired a majority stake in Ukraine's busiest cargo port despite ongoing war risks and proximity to Russian-occupied territories.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇫🇯 Israel Opens First Embassy in Fiji, Expanding Pacific Diplomatic Presence
item.description: Israel established its first embassy in Fiji as part of broader efforts to expand diplomatic footprint in the Pacific region, amid growing competition for influence in the area.

item.title: 🇪🇹⚡🚗 Ethiopia Goes Electric Following Gas Car Ban
item.description: Ethiopia banned gasoline-powered vehicles, pushing for widespread adoption of electric cars as the African nation seeks to reduce fuel imports and modernize its transportation sector.

item.title: 🇮🇳🐜 India's Cockroach Movement Gives Voice to Angry Youth
item.description: A grassroots movement in India is channeling youth frustration over unemployment and economic inequality, with protesters adopting the cockroach as a symbol of resilience against systemic corruption.

item.title: 🇸🇧🇨🇳 Solomon Islands New PM Vows to Review Secretive China Security Pact
item.description: Newly elected Prime Minister Jeremiah Manele announced his government will review a controversial security agreement with China, signaling potential shift in Pacific alliances amid US-China competition.

item.title: 🇲🇿🇿🇦 Mozambique Reports Five Citizens Killed in Xenophobic Attacks in South Africa
item.description: Mozambique government says five of its citizens were killed in xenophobic violence in neighboring South Africa, amid rising tensions over immigration and economic competition.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇷🇼⛪ Pope Leo Opens Paris Memorial Honoring Rwanda Genocide Victims
item.description: President Macron opened a monument in Paris acknowledging France responsibility in the Rwanda genocide, calling it a reconciliation milestone with victims memorial.

item.title: 🇹🇳 Tunisian Opposition Leader Ghannouchi Sentenced to Life in Prison
item.description: A Tunisian court sentenced Ennahdha leader Rached Ghannouchi to life imprisonment for "forming a terrorist alliance," along with dozens of other defendants receiving lengthy prison terms. The crackdown marks a major escalation against opposition figures in Tunisia.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Formally Requests 16-Year Extension of USMCA Trade Pact
item.description: Canada has formally requested a 16-year renewal of the USMCA free trade agreement, with the trade minister urging the three North American nations to extend the deal. The move comes ahead of the scheduled review period.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russian weapons plant ablaze after Ukrainian strike on Tambov aviation factory
item.description: Ukraine attacked the JSC Progress Plant in Michurinsk, which produces aviation and missile control systems for Russia's military. The strike marks continued Ukrainian pressure on Russian defense industry.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Clashes erupt in UK city over police handcuffing dying student
item.description: Protests broke out after police were caught on video handcuffing 18-year-old Henry Nowak as he lay dying. Officers are under investigation for racism after the incident in a British city.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇬🇧 China-UK diplomatic thaw as Beijing calls for stronger high-level exchanges
item.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi urged improved communication and alignment with Britain during talks, signaling a potential warming of relations between the two countries amid global turbulence.

item.title: ⛪ Notre Dame cathedral excavation unearths 1,700 years of history
item.description: Archaeologists discovered extraordinary treasures beneath Notre Dame during post-fire restoration, including medieval sculptures and Roman-era remains. The "dig of the century" reveals Paris's history from antiquity through the medieval period.
